channel.title: Home on Karso_sp
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Karso_sp

item.title: first blog post
item.description:
Heading 1

This is the first blog. Hello world.

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Cras eget sollicitudin massa. Nullam quis leo vitae velit cursus semper. Curabitur fringilla, nulla non suscipit convallis, dui justo condimentum nisi, sit amet cursus ligula nisi quis est. Morbi vel ante congue, pulvinar ipsum condimentum, scelerisque eros. Phasellus non vulputate quam. Proin et quam quis dolor lacinia euismod ac sed lacus. Fusce ullamcorper ex in pellentesque vulputate. Class aptent taciti sociosqu ad litora torquent per conubia nostra, per inceptos himenaeos. Pellentesque habitant morbi tristique senectus et netus et malesuada fames ac turpis egestas. Integer sem neque, consequat non eleifend eu, efficitur non diam. Donec eu dolor ac magna finibus fringilla mattis ac augue. Proin mattis, justo ut faucibus rutrum, massa ipsum pulvinar quam, non porta sapien est sit amet erat. Donec nisl orci, luctus et sem at, efficitur iaculis turpis. Donec sed quam et nibh pulvinar sollicitudin ut vitae metus.

item.title: Another Note on A blogdown Tutorial
item.description: I just discovered an awesome tutorial on blogdown written by Alison. I have to admit this is the best blogdown tutorial I have seen so far.

item.title: A Quick Note on Two Beautiful Websites
item.description:
To me, the two most impressive websites based on blogdown are:

  1. Rob J Hyndman’s personal website.
  2. Live Free or Dichotomize by Lucy and Nick et al.

I’m sure there will be more.

item.title: A Plain Markdown Post
item.description:
This sample post is mainly for blogdown users. If you do not use blogdown, you can skip the first section.

1. Markdown or R Markdown

This is a post written in plain Markdown (*.md) instead of R Markdown (*.Rmd). The major differences are:

  1. You cannot run any R code in a plain Markdown document, whereas in an R Markdown document, you can embed R code chunks (```{r});
  2. A plain Markdown post is rendered through Goldmark by default, and an R Markdown document is compiled by rmarkdown and Pandoc.

There are differences in syntax between Goldmark’s Markdown and Pandoc’s Markdown. For example, Goldmark does not support LaTeX math and Pandoc does. I have added the KaTeX support to this theme (i.e., hugo-xmin)

item.title: About This Site
item.description: I’m nobody who is interested in science, philosophy, and a lot other thingss, and this is my personal website where i would express myself through writing. The current internet and the whole ecosystem of it is just full of distraction, AI-slop, and attention-mining advertisement. The hope of creating this site is to fight those by creating this personal “digital garden”, thus i can be more thoughtful and intentional in consuming stuffs from the internet.
